title: Buddy: America's First Guide Dog
story: Paws tapped on pavement as Buddy, a German Shepherd, made history. In 1928, this brave canine became the first guide dog for a US citizen named Morris Frank. On April 25, Buddy led Frank through bustling streets, showing the world how dogs could be more than pets—they could be eyes for the blind. This groundbreaking partnership opened doors for countless visually impaired people, proving that with a faithful friend by their side, independence was possible.

Audiences were wowed by the innovative performances that brought new energy to the Great White Way.
activity_question: What type of dance was featured in the musical?
bonus_fact: The show's star, Savion Glover, was only 22 years old when it opened on Broadway.

title: Freda Payne's Golden Hit
story: Freda Payne's voice soared to new heights in 1970. On April 25, her signature single 'Band of Gold' hit the airwaves. The catchy tune, released by Invictus Records, quickly became a fan favorite. Payne's powerful vocals and the song's memorable melody helped it climb the charts, leaving a lasting mark on music history.
activity_question: What was the name of Freda Payne's signature single released on April 25, 1970?
bonus_fact: Invictus Records was founded by the famous songwriting team Holland-Dozier-Holland.

title: Nicaragua's Islands: A Shift in Control
story: Waves lapped at the shores of the Corn Islands as a big change swept over them. In 1971, on April 25, the United States' rights to these Caribbean gems off Nicaragua's coast came to an end. This wasn't just about two small islands, though. The US also lost its special access to build canals in Nicaragua. It marked a turning point, giving Nicaragua more say over its own land and waters.
activity_question: What did the United States lose rights to in Nicaragua?
activity_choices: Build canals and control Corn Islands
activity_answer: Build canals and control Corn Islands
follow_up_question: Why might a country want to control its own islands and waterways?
